clanmuramasa.quote: Let thy steps be slow and steady, on life's long journey.
clanmuramasa.description: They say that 'Faith can move mountains.' Indeed, the Muramasa people gain power through perseverance, diligently harvesting the land's resources, and crafting exquisite Samurai armor and weaponry.

yamato.description: Wide, rolling hills and large pond-filled grasslands. Default home of Clan Muramasa.

spring.description: Spring in Yamato Hills is a season of rebirth, lasting three months. Rains melt the snows, revealing new growth hidden underneath, and trees come back to life covered with colorful blossoms.

summer.description: Summer in Yamato Hills is a hot season, lasting three months. It is a time to relax and bask in the sun, but strong winds can bring thunderstorms or sandstorms.

autumn.description: Autumn in Yamato Hills is the harvest season, lasting three months. The trees turn to gold, and the grass prepares to retreat into its winter blanket, as the skies darken with storm clouds.

winter.description: Winter in Yamato Hills is a cold season, lasting three months. Many trees lose their leaves, and snow covers the land. The cold, biting winds are best countered by cozy gatherings around the hearth.

patterns.wood_blended_diagonal: A Pattern of Muesli, Shadow, Tobacco Brown, and Punga

dual_wield_knight.description: This Samurai currently prefers using a mainhand weapon with an off-hand weapon. Click to change!

clanmuramasa_archer.description: This Archer currently prefers wearing armor made by the Muramasa. Click to change!

bulletin_message.00:
Greetings to you, Clan Muramasa. My name is Amaterasu.
Your farmers can grow Rice on the land here.

Traditionally, Rice is planted on flooded areas. This can be done naturally near a lake or river, but water can also be placed where you choose by using these gifts:

"The Wet Stone:" An area can be filled with water by placing this summon stone that emits liquid.
"The Dry Stone:" A summon stone which can dry up nearby moisture, if there is too much in the way.

Hello again, Clan Muramasa. You have grown, through your dedication and resolve. I applaud you.

Now, I offer to you another gift, full of the natural magic of our world, Hearth. With this talisman, a member can tap into Hearth magic to alter the land, using Geomancy. Using the essence harvested from Hearthbud plants, they will create great things!

"Amaterasu disappears into the light."
Place these plants somewhere to harvest their essence:
A curious tanuki, with a small backpack approaches, delivering to us the "Imperial Shogunate Official Mail." As we read the letter from the Shogun, we remember how we came to be here.

open_letter.choice_001: [skip] We know what banner we want.

bulletin_message.00:
The Muramasa Clan once lived near the Eastern seaside, as an industrious group skilled in crafting exceptional steel weaponry, and bringing forth bountiful crop harvests from the fertile land.

They prospered for generations, fending off the evil forces that would invade their land from time to time.

Tired of its monsters being constantly defeated, the ferocious Squid Kami in the sea mounted a great invasion one day.

As it gained power, it dealt the final blow to the seaside town, by rumbling the earth and releasing a destructive Tsunami wave.

As the gang of monsters gained a foothold on the region, there were a few Muramasa survivors that escaped. They travelled as far as they could and have arrived to settle in this place now.

Clan Muramasa keeps hope within themselves, to rebuild, and resist the evil forces of this period. The Shogun is reaching out, to provide aid.

Clan Muramasa, the Shogun knows of your tragedy, and will support your rebuilding efforts. The Shogunate wishes to unify all of our people, to fight together against the evil forces rampaging throughout the lands.
Please name your new outpost, and choose a banner representative of your skills: you will receive a suitable bonus.

Be safe,
Shogun,
Eastern Imperial Shogunate
Apparently, Tanuki also brought some food items, and Tanuki's backpack contains a small satchel full of coins, labeled "Outpost Founding Incentive".
Once the outpost founding forms are filled and stored in their backpack, Tanuki starts on his journey back to the Imperial Shogunate headquarters.
The messenger tanuki returns, with another letter from the Shogun.

Congratulations on your Founding!

Your Outpost is now officially in the Imperial record, and I trust that you're well on your way to rebuilding a prosperous new town!
To help you achieve this worthy goal, we are extending the roadways out from the Capital, encouraging brave travellers, and merchants to head to [str(i18n_data.town_name)].

Conversely, we expect you to start considering another growth stage to your Outpost. Your renowned prowess in producing weaponry to resist the monsters is valuable to the Empire, as is your skill in pulling forth abundant minerals, and food produce from this land.

Be safe!
Shogun
Eastern Imperial Shogunate


P.S. A small gift for you, for good luck ✨
A Hearth of Makers, on the other hand, is for those who believe that a town's value is its work, and that its work is best measured in gold.
Cheer, Glory, or Makers - the choice is entirely yours. Think of what matters most to your Settlement, and follow that beacon into the future!

As you know, Hearths have been the cornerstone of Settlements all the way back to the old Phoenix Empire.

If not for their glow, brave new settlers would never find their way easily, and our society would have scattered long ago. The type of Hearth also says much about what a Settlement values.
For instance, a Hearth of Cheer is for those who believe that a town's value is found in being a happy home for its citizens.
Finally, a Hearth of Glory is for those who believe that there comes a time to be brave, and don protective armor and weaponry, to protect the land by force.

Hello! This time I am personally visiting Clan Muramasa, to view your Outpost. We are very impressed with your Outpost's progress, and have decided that it's time to officially update your status to that of a 'Settlement'.

Before we can do that, though, it is customary to choose a Hearth to represent your town.

intro.choice_001: [skip] We know what Hearth we want.

bulletin_message.00:
I am making arrangements to visit your Settlement myself, and when I do, I will be bringing along several individuals who have expressed interest in guiding up and coming Settlements like yours. These are highly regarded members of our society, so be sure to have your best meals ready!

Bless you!
Shogun
Eastern Imperial Shogunate

Dear [str(i18n_data.town_name)]-ites,

I have received many reports from travellers singing your Settlement all kinds of praises. I am so glad that your determination to grow in strength and skill is succeeding!
To progress to the next stage of your Settlement, you can receive guidance from some specialists of the Eastern Empire, who can help with crossing that final bridge to becoming a Town.
The Shogun is getting in touch with us again!

Hello, [str(i18n_data.town_name)]!

I'm very glad to get to see your Settlement here today!

Now, to the matters at hand. Here are the potential guides I mentioned in my last letter. Please take a moment to hear what they have to say.

Greetings again, brothers and sisters! I am Amaterasu, here to visit you all. The land is full of mysteries and secrets.
Being in tune with the surroundings, I can offer special blessings, and teach how to craft some lovely decorative items.

In return for guidance, I typically ask a Settlement to build a Cleyera Shrine, evocative of the nature and friendly creatures that can be found, and reflection of our exploration and learning.
Once the shrine is blessed, it will spread its magical effects among your populace, empowering them in strange and mysterious ways.

Dedicating a Cleyera Shrine will grant all of your townsfolk a +1 boost to the Mind, Body, and Spirit attributes.

Come here, people! I'm Shokuzen, a Master Crafter of the Za Guild, and an admirer of this budding little Settlement of yours. So efficient! So promising!
The goods that you are trading with the other Clans are so helpful in growing our Empire.

Shokuzen examines and admires the finely crafted furniture and stonework.

As you know, the Za Guild is an important agency of Crafters amongst the Empire, so I'm sure you aren't surprised to see me here.
We would love to have you join the Guild - I think your Settlement can produce and export some of the finest goods Clan Muramasa has to offer, with your highly skilled artisans.

Dedicating a Hinamatsuri Shrine will grant the ability to create Masterwork-quality furniture and decorations, which are considerably better than those of Excellent quality.

Hey! I am Oni! Don't be afraid, by my appearance... I turned away from the Squid Kami's army of darkness a long time ago, and a Shinto Priest helped to purge out the evil within me.
I have joined the Shogun's defence force, and can offer military training in various self defense techniques. Your deadly blades need to be wielded against the evil invasions!

We think your people have shown that they have the strength needed to protect this Settlement, and even some of your neighbors.
So, we'd like you to build a Kami Shrine to be a beacon of strength in these lands, and in return I will share some of our unique weapon and armor schematics.

Dedicating the Kami Shrine will grant you recipes for new, highest-tier weapons.

So! Now you've met everyone, each a specialist with much to offer. I don't want to influence your choice, but I can assure you they're all very powerful and can help your town achieve its full potential.

I'll leave the plans for each of the Shrines and whatnot with your Carpenter.

It's been a pleasure visiting, and I'm looking forward to seeing you again at the dedication ceremony!

guildmaster.name: Hinamatsuri Shrine
guildmaster.description:
This Township shall be known for its productivity, wealth, and craftsmanship.
  • Allows crafting Masterwork quality items
  • Unlocks homely decoration recipes

deity.name: Cleyera Shrine
deity.description:
This Township shall seek to learn the secrets of the world, in service of Amaterasu.
  • Blesses all citizens with +1 to Mind, Body, and Spirit
  • Unlocks gold decoration recipes

valor.name: Kami Shrine
valor.description:
This Township shall be dedicated to the protection of the whole surrounding region.
  • Unlocks legendary weapon recipes
  • Unlocks exotic decoration recipes

shrine_upgrade_deity.title: Township Established

shrine_upgrade_deity.reward_message_ctd: For learning the mysteries of the world. The town is hereby eligible for:

shrine_upgrade_guildmaster.reward_message: By decree of the Imperial Shogunate, we recognize:
shrine_upgrade_guildmaster.reward_message_ctd: For excellence in craftsmanship. The town is hereby eligible for:

recipe_bulletins.recipe_unlock_potter: New pot, floral, and workbench decoration recipes for Potter!
recipe_bulletins.recipe_unlock_mason: New fountain decoration recipes for Mason!

shrine_upgrade_valor.reward_message: By decree of the Imperial Shogunate, we recognize:
shrine_upgrade_valor.reward_message_ctd: For acts of incredible valor in combat. The town is hereby eligible for:

achievements.003: Weaver Banners, Throws, and Cushions Recipes

recipe_bulletins.recipe_unlock_weaver: New banner, throw, and cushion recipes for Weaver!

bulletin_message.00:
Of course, with our backing, everyone will know that when you call something a 'Masterwork', you mean it!

I have no doubt that traders will soon be going out of their way to pay your Town a visit. I'd expect lots of company!

Alright, enough with all this business talk - I'm going to find some Sake, and get to dancing!

Surprised? You're only as old as you act!

(Shokuzen nods, mumbling to himself. 'Hmm... yes, I suppose this will do nicely, nicely indeed.')

Oh! Muramasa Clan, you have chosen well to sign on as part of the Za Guild's network, and we are certainly glad to have you!

msg1.bulletin_title: Shokuzen leans in, examining the Shrine

bulletin_message.00:
Ha! I guess that's true!

So, I have brought the ancient scrolls instructing the process to forge the feared weapons of power - pass them along to your crafters, so they can try and work out how to put 'em together.

msg2.choice_000: I'm sure they'll figure it out!

bulletin_message.00:
No doubt!

Alright, I've been on my feet all day, time to sit down and feast! Congratulations!

Wow, I bet this can be seen from miles off! Those goblins will be running scared before long!

Its good that you received my training, we will strike down the monsters, and create a peaceful Empire!

Henceforth, this blessed Shrine will bestow a little of my power upon each of you.

Ponder it. Take that curiosity out into the world with you as you trade, or explore, or welcome strangers into your Town, hoping to attain some tantalizing new knowledge.
Of course, we at the Temple will expect a regular report of any goings on. That can wait, though - for now, it's time for all of us to eat, drink, and be merry!

Blessings to you, sisters and brothers. What a suitable shrine you've built!

I especially enjoy the cute kitty statue!

Wonderful, wonderful! Your Town certainly does the Muramasa name proud!

Thank you for throwing such a nice reception for our guests, and I.

This monument we're celebrating about is special, by the way - a Town's founding imbues a special sort of magic into it.

Building more statues and such can serve decorative purposes, but they won't have the same sort of glow, if you know what I mean.

(Yap! Yap!)

Yes, yes, Tanuki, I know, it's time to go.

I'd love to stay longer, but I must continue in my mission to unite the Clans of the Empire.

Congratulations on all your success, and thanks for not having ever made too much paperwork for me.

Blessings, friend! Now that you are thriving and established, we'd like to remind you that the Imperial Shogunate is assisting all new Clan Settlements!

In fact, if you'd like to establish another town, we'd be happy to sponsor a large embarkment crew that can carry a few of your citizens from here, as well as some extra goods and records. Just let us know when you're ready!

Re-embarking allows you to carry over up to 3 citizens, 10 items, and all your unlocked recipes when starting a new town.

banner_trading_ghost.description: How you know you've come home.
banner_trading_ghost.town_bonus_description:
This settlement will be a bustling hub of trade.
  • Roads give 3x their normal speed boost
  • Your items sell for 50% more
  • Traders will bring 2x the goods and Gold

banner_trading_token_iconic.description: A shred from the Banner of Trading, carrying along its blessing.

banner_mining_ghost.description: How you know you've come home
banner_mining_ghost.town_bonus_description:
This settlement will grow strong through earth and steel.
  • Mining gives 50% more Ore, Stone, and Clay
  • Hearthlings no longer mind cramped spaces

banner_mining_token_iconic.description: A shred from the Banner of Mining, carrying along its blessing.

banner_growing_ghost.description: How you know you've come home
banner_growing_ghost.town_bonus_description:
This settlement shall be at one with the environment.
  • Trees produce 25% more Wood
  • Plants and Crops both grow 25% faster
  • Plants have 2x their normal Appeal
